step.cmd: Before starting the work instruction activity, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Do not begin these tasks without confirmation that personnel directly involved have completed the required training associated with the work instruction activity and have reviewed this document.
Gather necessary tools and equipment as identified below in the Equipment Section.
Wear and use additional Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and safety equipment required for this specific work instruction.
Obey all applicable safety requirements and procedures.
Notify appropriate personnel of the activity and estimated time needed to complete the tasks.
Follow all Lockout Tagout procedures when work instruction activity requires isolating energy in the designated area. Verify energy is isolated before starting work.

step.cmd: Prerequisite

substep.cmd: Prior to this work instruction, ensure container handling was properly set up according to INS-03-055 Container Handling – Tiama – Multistation 3.

step.cmd: For proper setup, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: There are two screens located on the Multi 3
User Interface Screen
Setup Screen

step.cmd: Parameters Adjustment
Container Images Display
For setup for CSBC-LO, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Jog a setup container into the CSBC-LO station.
Login in using Job Change password
Press the CSBC icon on the operation screen.
Click on the adjustment button.
Press the camera button
Select and install optional equipment according to MSDR INS-07-038 Parts List.
Select the image alone button.
Center the container under the camera center using the jog switch and view on the screen.
Adjust and verify the light source and camera positions using the job history or if a new job, follow these steps:
Adjust the light source at a distance of 5 mm (3/16 inch) above the finish or greater.

step.cmd: The height depends on the type of light source used and can be up to 50 mm.

substep.cmd: Adjust height of camera using the adjustment crank until the finish is inside the reference blue lines. Be careful not to collide with the camera.
If required physically align the camera and light source over the center of the finish.
Adjust light source and camera until a “best possible” image is obtained.
Adjust the light source opening using the adjustable diaphragm or aperture plates according to MSDR INS-038 Parts List to prevent unwanted reflections and improve contrast.

step.cmd: To adjust optical settings, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Turn on the Oscilloscope and press the Source button.
Open the aperture completely and reduce the opening by 1 step.
Adjust the light level intensity to achieve 50-60% scale of the surface of the finish with the shortest integration time as possible.
Adjust the camera focus to achieve the best possible image.
Balance light sources to for the most consistent gray color across both zones as much as possible.
Press the green check mark to close the menu.

step.cmd: To set up distances, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Jog container back into the CSBC-LO photocell position. Adjust height and sensitivity of the photocell. Jog a container left and right to ensure proper operation of the photocell.
Open the Distances menu and press the Distances button.
Close doors
Change machine to Automatic Mode using the switch on the front of the machine.
Turn on conveyor
Pass a setup container through the machine to capture an image.
Turn on Cross Hairs by checking the Target box. Use the + and - to adjust the image to where it can be seen in the center of the Crosshair.
Pass containers through machine and readjust as required.
Repeat process until centered.
Press the green check to close the menu.

step.cmd: To set zone and threshold parameters, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Press the zone parameters button.
Select the outer circle and adjust size and location using the arrows and size box on the screen until the circle is slightly larger than the outside of the finish.
Press the another circle to switch between circles. Selected circle will be red.
Select internal circle and adjust size and location using the arrows and size box on the screen, until the circle is slightly smaller than the inside of the finish.
Press the another circle to switch between circles.
Press the green check to save and exit.

step.cmd: To set up localization, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Click on the localization button on the user interface screen to open registration screen.
Confirm auto checkbox is checked.
Press the localization auto button.
Size and threshold will readjust automatically
After pressing localization auto, review the localization results.
The outer box will change from red to green when localization is successful.
Ensure the localization circle has adjusted correctly to the outside and inside of the finish.
If result detection is
OK=successful
NOK=unsuccessful
Treatment Duration (processing time) should be as short as possible and not exceed 10 milliseconds.
Correlation ratio should be at least 85% (100% means the software has ideally identified the finish)
Transition indicates which method of detection is used (black to white or white to black).
Optimum localization diameter is calculated.
Close window by pressing the Green check box.
If Auto Localization was not successful adjust localization manually.

step.cmd: To set localization manually, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Manual localization
Deselect Auto check box.
Press threshold button.
Confirm the color radio button is selected by default.
Adjust the threshold so the finish circle is unbroken and sharp.
The outer box on the setup screen will change from Red to green when the circle size and location is good.
Press green check box to save and exit.
Adjust size of circle to external or internal diameter of the finish. Readjust position of the circle as needed by using arrows.
If inner diameter finish is chosen then transition method must be forced in Expert Localization menu. (“B=>N”)
If external diameter finish is chosen then transition method must be forced in Expert Localization menu. (“N=>B”)
Review localization results and repeat adjustment as needed.
Press green check box to save and exit.
If desired localization results are not achieved, switch to expert mode
Once in expert mode, select threshold and repeat threshold section above.
Press zone and repeat zone section above. Adjust localization circle position and size to the desired location.
Select the transition method depending on which area of the finish was selected (“B=>N” internal or “N=>B” external) – This will force transition method for both manual and automatic modes.
Correlation checkbox – should always checked (may vary with software versions).
Correlation Threshold – Set to 85% or higher.
White Diameter Correlation – default is 2 (adjustment is not recommended)
Dark Diameter Correlation – default is 2 (adjustment is not recommended)
Diameter Tolerance – default is 10% to accommodate ovalization.
Vertical Jump – adjust if needed. The lower number can improve registration, but will increase processing time, and reduce throughput.
Transitions Strength (contrast) – this value can be changed to increase contrast. Typically set between 10-15.
Press test, analyze results and repeat until successful. Press the green check to save and close.
Click Dynamic Registration
Turn on the conveyor
Pass multiple containers through the machine.
Review all registration results. If not successful, return to localization setup sections. Press anywhere on the screen to exit dynamic registration screen.

step.cmd: To set inspection zones and sensitivities, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Click on the Defect Parameters button on the User Interface screen.
Turn on all defects that will be inspected for on this screen, according to the MSDR INS-07-031 Minimum Inspection for Tiama Channels.
Possible Defect types are listed on the setup screen
Click on the green check button to exit the screen.
Obtain a trapped image of a commercial container or ensure a trapped image is already in memory.

step.cmd: To set the Line Over Finish inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Click the Adjustments button for Line Over Finish.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the internal or external circle to be adjusted by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust inside Line Over Finish circle to the inner edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Adjust the outside Line Over Finish circle to the outer edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Select Filter 1D
Click on Threshold
Select color (should be selected by default)
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.

step.cmd: If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Refer to the two horizontal bands at the top and bottom of the setup screen, these correspond to the “unrolled” view of the finish (Dependent on software version)
There should be minimal “noise” in the bands.

substep.cmd: Click the green check box to exit.
Press the test button.
Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has passed inspection. If the defect is not detected the outer box will change to green.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has passed inspection.
If the defect is detected, the outer box will change to red.
Line (xx-xx) xx-x*
(xx-xx) are the length and elongation thresholds
xx-x is the result
* means there is a LINE defect, which is also shown on the container image screen
If the number exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
COR should be close to100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the White Defects inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: “White Defects” can detect Blisters or other white objects on the finish.
Click the Adjustments button for White Defects.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the circle to be adjusted by using the mouse to click on the circle or by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust inside White Defect circle to the inner edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Adjust the outside White Defect circle to the outer edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.
If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Minimum defect energy: the lowest threshold of energy for a white defect. If the defect energy exceeds the entered value, the machine considers there is a defect and rejects the container.
The size of the defect window can be between 2 for small defects and 100 for large defects.
Press the test button.

Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has not been rejected. If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has not been rejected.
DB (xxxx) xxxx*
(xxxx) is the defect’s energy threshold.
xxxx is the result of the energy measurement
* means that an energy defect has been detected.
COR should be close to 100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
If the result exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the Internal Defects inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: “Internal Defects” will detect Blisters, Dark Object, and Dirty Finishes.
Click the Adjustments button for Internal Defects.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the circle to be adjusted by using the mouse to click on the circle or by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust the circles to frame the inner section of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.

step.cmd: If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Minimum defect energy: the lowest threshold of energy for an Internal defect. If the defect energy exceeds the entered value, the machine considers there is a defect and rejects the container.
The size of the defect window can be between 2 for small defects and 100 for large defects.

substep.cmd: Press the test button.

Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has not been rejected. If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has not been rejected.
The result of the Internal Defects process is displayed. Analyze the inspection results.
Re-adjust Internal Defects parameters if needed until proper detection is obtained.
If the Internal Defect is detected, the outer box will change to red.
If the Internal Defect is not detected the outer box will change to green.
DB (xxxx) xxxx*
(xxxx) is the defect’s energy threshold.
xxxx is the result of the energy measurement
* means that an energy defect has been detected.
COR should be close to 100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
If the result exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the External Defects inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: “External Defects” will detect Blisters, Bright Objects, and Chipped Finishes.
Click the Adjustments button for External Defects.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the circle to be adjusted by using the mouse to click on the circle or by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust the circles to frame the outer section of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.

step.cmd: If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Minimum defect energy: the lowest threshold of energy for an Internal defect. If the defect energy exceeds the entered value, the machine considers there is a defect and rejects the container.
The size of the defect window can be between 2 for small defects and 100 for large defects.

substep.cmd: Press the test button.

Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has not been rejected. If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has not been rejected.
Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has passed inspection. If the defect is not detected the outer box will change to green.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has passed inspection.
If the defect is detected, the outer box will change to red.
DB (xxxx) xxxx*
(xxxx) is the defect’s energy threshold.
xxxx is the result of the energy measurement
* means that an energy defect has been detected.
COR should be close to 100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
If the result exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the Black Defects inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: “Black Defects” can detect Open Blisters, Unfilled Finish, Dirty Finish, or other Black objects on the finish.
Click the Adjustments button for Black Defects.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the circle to be adjusted by using the mouse to click on the circle or by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust inside Black Defect circle to the inner edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Adjust the outside Black Defect circle to the outer edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.

step.cmd: If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Minimum defect energy: the lowest threshold of energy for a Black defect. If the defect energy exceeds the entered value, the machine considers there is a defect and rejects the container.
The size of the defect window can be between 2 for small defects and 100 for large defects.

substep.cmd: Press the test button.

Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has not been rejected. If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has not been rejected.
DB (xxxx) xxxx*
(xxxx) is the defect’s energy threshold.
xxxx is the result of the energy measurement* means that an energy defect has been detected.
COR should be close to 100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
If the result exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the Sugary Finish inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: “Sugary Defects” can detect Sugary or Crizzle defect on the top of the sealing surface.
Click the Adjustments button for Sugary Defects.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the circle to be adjusted by using the mouse to click on the circle or by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust inside Sugary Defect circle to the inner edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Adjust the outside Sugary Defect circle to the outer edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.
If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Minimum defect energy: the lowest threshold of energy for a Sugary defect. If the defect energy exceeds the entered value, the machine considers there is a defect and rejects the container.
The size of the defect window can be between 2 for small defects and 100 for large defects.
Press the test button.

Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has not been rejected. If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has not been rejected.
DB (xxxx) xxxx*
(xxxx) is the defect’s energy threshold.
xxxx* means that an energy defect has been detected.
If the number exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Pass a good (commercial) container through the machine to make sure it is NOT rejected.
Analyze the results on the Test Screen. Compare results on the Test screen to make sure the defect readings are not too close to the defect set- points.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the traverse contraction (thin finish) inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Click the Adjustments button for traverse contraction.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the internal or external circle to be adjusted by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust inside circle to the inner edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Adjust the outside circle to the outer edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Click on Threshold
Select color (should be selected by default)
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.

step.cmd: If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.

substep.cmd: Refer to the two horizontal bands at the top and bottom of the setup screen, these correspond to the“unrolled” view of the finish.

step.cmd: There should be minimal “noise” in the bands.

substep.cmd: Click the green check box to exit.
Press the test button.
Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has passed inspection. If the Line Over Finish defect is not detected the outer box will change to green.
Re-adjust Line Over Finish parameters if needed until commercial container has passed inspection.
If the Line Over Finish defect is detected, the outer box will change to red.
Line (xx-xx) xx-x*
(xx-xx) are the length and elongation thresholds
xx-x is the result
* means there is a LINE defect, which is also shown on the container image screen
If the number exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
COR should be close to 100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: To set the deformation (ovalization) inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Click the Adjustments button for deformation.
Press the zone button to open the zone screen.
Choose the internal or external circle to be adjusted by pressing the “Another Circle” button.
Adjust inside deformation circle to the inner edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Adjust the outside deformation circle to the outer edge of the finish using the + and – buttons to adjust size or by entering a size number.
Press the green button to save and close.
Click on Threshold
Select color (should be selected by default)
Verify parameters from job history (loaded during container handling)
Ensure these parameters are compliant with MSDR INS-07-033 Inspection Device Setup Parameters for Tiama Equipment.
If no job history exists, parameters must be entered as specified in the MSDR.
Click the green check box to exit.
Press the test button.

Analyze the inspection results to ensure the commercial container has not been rejected. If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
Re-adjust parameters if needed until commercial container has not been rejected.
Line (xx-xx) xx-x*
(xx-xx) are the length and elongation thresholds
xx-x is the result
* means there is a LINE defect, which is also shown on the container image screen
If the number exceeds the threshold it is a defect.
COR should be close to 100% as possible. COR below 85% is unacceptable.
TPS is the total processing time in milliseconds.
Use the mouse to click any place on the screen to exit the test screen.
Press the green check box to exit the screen.

step.cmd: For Dynamic Test, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Click Dynamic Test.
Run a challenge sample through the machine.
Analyze the inspection results to ensure the challenge sample has been rejected If no defect is detected, the outer box will show green. If a defect has been detected, the outer box will show red.
If challenge sample was rejected, run challenge sample for the next inspection(s) in dynamic mode.
If challenge sample was not rejected, re-adjust parameters until challenge sample has been rejected as specified in next section.
Click on any screen to exit the dynamic test.

step.cmd: For challenge samples that do not reject, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Press the parameters button.
Select the adjustments button for the defect to be adjusted.
If line over finish was not rejected, adjust any or all of the following:
minimum length
elongation
threshold
Press Test to verify result. If not successful, repeat step. Press green check to save and exit.
If any other defect was not rejected, adjust any or all of the following:

step.cmd: Minimum energy
Size
Threshold

substep.cmd: Press Test to verify result. If not successful, repeat step. Press green check to save and exit.
Press green test to save and exit.
Open the operations menu
Review and adjust results during production and update parameters as needed.
Open Thresholds from the Operations menu and adjust parameters to ensure challenge sample is rejected.

step.cmd: Upon completion of tasks, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Confirm the designated work area is clear and communicate to personnel the intent to restart energy.
Perform basic housekeeping duties. Clean up the work space, tools and equipment, dispose of trash, put tools and equipment in assigned area.
Notify appropriate personnel that the work instruction activity has been completed.
Record and report findings and results.
Follow appropriate instructions for notification of findings and results relating to specifications, targets and/or reaction limits.
